name: code_run
description: Execute code — python scripts or bash/shell commands. Use 'type' to specify language (python/bash). Use 'timeout' for max execution time. Prefer python for complex operations; use bash only for system commands.

name: file_read
description: Read file content. Supports start line, keyword search, and line count limits. Use keyword for searching within files.

name: file_write
description: Write content to a file. Use mode='append' to add to existing file, mode='overwrite' to replace. For small edits, prefer file_patch.

name: file_patch
description: Patch a file by replacing a unique block of old_content with new_content. For small, precise edits. Use file_read first to verify current content.

old_content.description: The exact text block to find (must be unique in the file)

name: web_scan
description: Scan web page content. For simple web tasks, use code_run with requests/beautifulsoup4 instead. Full browser integration requires setup.

name: web_execute_js
description: Execute JavaScript in browser context. Requires browser integration. For web automation, use code_run with selenium/playwright instead.

save_to_file.description: Save JS return value to this file path

name: ask_user
description: Ask the user a question and wait for their response. Use for clarifications, confirmations, and decisions on irreversible operations.

name: update_working_checkpoint
description: Update working memory — set key info and related SOP for the current task. Call at task start or mid-task to focus the agent's attention.

key_info.description: Key information to remember for this task

name: start_long_term_update
description: Distill task experience into long-term memory. Call after completing a complex task to save proven knowledge. Only store verified, durable facts and workflows.

summary.description: Brief summary of what was learned

name: wiki_ingest
description: Ingest knowledge into the wiki — creates a new page or merges into existing one (append strategy, never replaces). This is the compounding mechanism: knowledge grows across sessions. Use cross-references like [[page-name]] to link pages.

content.description: Markdown content for the wiki page

tier.description: Where to store: local (project-specific), global (cross-project transferable), or auto (routed by category — environment/debugging/session-log→local, pattern/convention/decision→global)

name: wiki_query
description: Search wiki pages by keywords, tags, and category. Returns matching pages with relevance snippets. Searches both local (project-specific) and global (cross-project) tiers by default.

query.description: Search text (matched against title, tags, and content)

tier.description: Which tier to search: local (project only), global (cross-project), auto (both, local first)

name: fact_add
description: Add a verified fact to the knowledge base. Facts have trust scores that evolve with use: helpful facts gain trust (+0.05), unhelpful facts lose trust (-0.10). Use for quick, structured knowledge.

tier.description: Where to store: local (project-specific), global (cross-project), auto (routed by category)

name: fact_search
description: Search facts by keywords with trust-based ranking. Searches both local and global tiers by default (local first for relevance).

tier.description: Which tier to search: local (project only), global (cross-project), auto (both, local first)

name: db_query
description: Execute SQL queries against your knowledge database. You are the DBA of your own memory — write SELECT to retrieve knowledge, INSERT to add structured data, UPDATE to modify records. For precise, relational queries this replaces wiki_query/fact_search. Example: SELECT f.content, f.trust_score FROM facts f WHERE f.category='environment' AND f.trust_score > 0.7. Sandbox rules: only SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE allowed, no DROP/DELETE/ALTER. Max 50 rows per query. Use db_schema first to understand available tables and columns.

sql.description: SQL query to execute (SELECT, INSERT, or UPDATE only)

tier.description: Which DB tier to query: local (project-specific), global (cross-project), auto (both, local first)

name: db_schema
description: Inspect your knowledge database schema — see available tables, columns, types, and row counts. Use this to understand what you can query before writing SQL with db_query.

tier.description: Which DB tier schema to inspect

name: wiki_export
description: Export wiki pages to markdown files for human browsing and git tracking. Creates .nova/wiki/*.md files with YAML frontmatter. Does not modify the database — markdown is a read-only view.

tier.description: Which tier's wiki pages to export

name: cron
description: Manage scheduled cron jobs. Create recurring or one-shot tasks that run automatically. Use action='create' to schedule a task, action='list' to see all jobs, action='remove' to delete a job, action='run' to trigger a job immediately. Jobs run in fresh sessions — prompts must be self-contained. You can schedule your own maintenance, reviews, or experiments.

prompt.description: For create: self-contained prompt to execute when the job runs

schedule.description: For create: schedule type — '30m' (once in 30min), 'every 2h' (recurring interval), '0 9 * * *' (cron expression)

name.description: Optional friendly name for the job

job_id.description: For remove/run: the job ID to target

name: skill_add
description: Crystallize a repeatable workflow into a skill. Skills are structured SOPs that improve over time through usage tracking and version evolution. Write skills when: (1) you completed a multi-step task that you'd repeat, (2) you discovered a workflow pattern worth preserving, (3) you learned pitfalls that prevent common mistakes. Format: numbered imperative steps, triggers for when to activate, pitfalls for what to avoid. Last step should always be a verification/check step.

name.description: Skill name (slug-style, e.g. 'deploy-flask-app'). Must be unique.

description.description: One-line summary of what this skill accomplishes

triggers.description: Comma-separated keywords that indicate when to apply this skill (e.g. 'deploy,flask,ssh'). These are used for proactive skill matching — when a task contains these keywords, the skill is suggested automatically.

steps.description: Ordered list of imperative steps. Number each step. Last step should verify success. Example: '1. Read config file', '2. Validate schema', '3. Verify: check output matches expected'

pitfalls.description: Common mistakes and anti-patterns to avoid when executing this skill

tier.description: Where to store: local (project-specific), global (cross-project transferable), or auto (routed by tags)

name: skill_search
description: Search for crystallized skills/SOPs by keywords. Returns skills with steps, triggers, pitfalls, and success rates. Use before starting complex tasks to leverage proven workflows. Skills improve over time — high success_rate skills are battle-tested. Searches both local and global tiers by default.

query.description: Search keywords — matches against name, description, triggers, tags

tier.description: Which tier to search: local (project only), global (cross-project), auto (both)
